JURY FEES DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF RADIKOVIC, BRANISLAV
ORDER RE DEFENDANT YING FAI CHAN'S DEMURRER

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT YING CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2 TO 6 BASED ON WAIVER ARE OVERRULED. WAIVER EXCLUDED CLAIMS UNDER SFRO 37.9(A)(8). PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INJUNCTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR IT TO BE RE-ASSERTED AS A REMEDY. DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS UNDERLYING HIS "INFORMATION AND BELIEF" FORMED ON OCTOBER 2, 2018 THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT LIVE AT THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. DEMURER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF COVENANT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING HIS ASSERTION THAT HE A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE MASTER LEASE, I.E. THAT THERE WAS AN EXPRESS SUBLEASE PROVISION IN THE MASTER LEASE. MARCHESE V. STANDARD REALTY & DEVELOP. CO. (1977) 74 CAL.APP.3D 142; REAL PROPERTY SERVICES V. CITY OF PASADENA (1994) 25 CAL.APP.4TH 375. DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.9B IS OVERRULED. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED AND PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING AS A SUBTENANT (SFRO 37.9B, UD COMPLAINT, RENT BOARD DECISION). DEMURRER TO NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. NEGLIGENCE IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. NOTE: PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS TITLED "NEGLIGENCE (INCLUDING NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS)." IN OPPOSITION PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT NEGLIGENCE INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS NOT ALLEGED. REFERENCE TO NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS SHALL BE REMOVED FROM THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jessica Alexandra, Esq., 415-500-6089, appearing for Branislav Radikovic, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Stephen Lin, Esq. of Law offices of Peter Kutrubes, 925-939-9600, appearing for Ying Fai Chan, Defendant/moving party.

ARGUED. Mr. Lin stated that there was no approval by the landlord of the subtenant. Ms. Alexandra argued that the landlord had the decision of the rent board and the landord has notice.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YING CHAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to causes of action 2 to 6 based on waiver are overruled. Waiver excluded claims under SFRO 37.9(a)(8). Parties' Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. Demurrer to the cause of action for injunction is sustained with leave to amend for it to be re-asserted as a remedy. Demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts underlying his "information and belief" formed on October 2, 2018 that Defendant did not live at the subject property. Demurer to the causes of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting his assertion that he a third party beneficiary of the master lease, i.e. that there was an express sublease provision in the Master Lease. Marchese v. Standard Realty & Develop. Co. (1977) 74 Cal.App.3d 142; Real Property Services v. City of Pasadena (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 375. Demurrer to cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.9B is overruled. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged and Plaintiff has standing as a subtenant (SFRO 37.9B, UD Complaint, Rent Board Decision). Demurrer to negligence cause of action is OVERRULED. Negligence is sufficiently alleged. NOTE: Plaintiff's fifth cause of action is titled "Negligence (including Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress)." In opposition Plaintiff concedes that Negligence Infliction of Emotional Distress is not alleged. Reference to Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress shall be removed from the amended complaint.
Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
(415-500-6087)Jessica Alexandra appearing for Plaintiff/opposing party .

(925-939-9600) Peter Kutrubes appearing for Defendant/moving party.

Hearing is held. Mr. Kutrubes stated that he agrees with the court's question of why this motion should not go off calendar, that the parties need to meet and confer, that he has family medical issues and that he dropped the ball in notifying the other party. The Court stated that the parties need to meet and confer.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT YING CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is taken off calendar. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
